verses.text: The priests the Levites, and all the tribe of Levi, shall have no part nor inheritance with Israel: they shall eat the offerings of the LORD made by fire, His inheritance. They have no inheritance among their brethren; the LORD is their inheritance. Guidance: Said three times in three verses — Levi gets no land, gets the LORD. The repetition is not editorial; it is theology. The most landless tribe holds the richest deed. Their dinner came from God's altar, their security from God's presence. When God is your inheritance, no other holding is essential.

The priests the Levites, and all the tribe of Levi, shall have no part nor inheritance with Israel: they shall eat the offerings of the LORD made by fire, His inheritance. They have no inheritance among their brethren; the LORD is their inheritance. Guidance: Said three times in three verses — Levi gets no land, gets the LORD. The repetition is not editorial; it is theology. The most landless tribe holds the richest deed. Their dinner came from God's altar, their security from God's presence. When God is your inheritance, no other holding is essential.

This shall be the priest's due from those that offer a sacrifice, ox or sheep: shoulder, two cheeks, maw. The firstfruit of thy corn, wine, oil, first fleece of thy sheep. The LORD hath chosen him out of all thy tribes, to stand to minister in the name of the LORD, him and his sons for ever. Guidance: Specific cuts of meat to specific people. The priest didn't have a salary; he had a share of every sacrifice. Worship paid the worship-leaders. And the first wool of every spring went to the man who couldn't grow a fleece himself.

If a Levite come from any of thy gates out of all Israel, where he sojourned, come with all the desire of his mind unto the place the LORD shall choose; then he shall minister in the name of the LORD, as all his brethren. They shall have like portions to eat, beside that which cometh of the sale of his patrimony. Guidance: A migrant Levite gets the same plate at the central table. No second-tier ministers. The priesthood is a brotherhood, not a guild. Whatever family inheritance he sold to come, he eats with the rest.

When thou art come into the land, thou shalt not learn to do after the abominations of those nations. There shall not be found any that maketh his son or daughter pass through the fire, that useth divination, an observer of times, an enchanter, a witch, a charmer, a consulter with familiar spirits, a wizard, a necromancer. All that do these are an abomination unto the LORD. Thou shalt be perfect with the LORD thy God. Guidance: Nine occult practices named, plus child sacrifice. God refuses Israel access to every magical shortcut. He has His own channels of communication — prophet, priest, urim. The walk with God is by His revelation, not by ours through smoke or bones.

Those nations hearkened unto diviners; but as for thee, the LORD hath not suffered thee so to do. The LORD will raise up unto thee a Prophet from thy brethren, like unto me; unto Him ye shall hearken. I will put my words in His mouth. Whosoever will not hearken unto my words which He shall speak in my name, I will require it of him. Guidance: God answers Horeb's request for a mediator with a promise that echoes thirteen centuries forward. Like-unto-Moses — a prophet who carries God's words and holds the people's fear. Peter and Stephen would later say: this was Jesus. The chapter that bans divination opens onto the most certain word a people ever received.

The prophet which shall presume to speak a word in my name which I have not commanded, or shall speak in the name of other gods — that prophet shall die. If thou say in thine heart, How shall we know the word which the LORD hath not spoken? When a prophet speaketh in the name of the LORD, if the thing follow not — that is not the LORD's word; the prophet hath spoken it presumptuously: thou shalt not be afraid of him. Guidance: A working test for any claimed prophecy: does it come true? False prophets are not unstoppable — their predictions expose them. Don't fear someone whose words don't land. Time is the courtroom; history is the verdict.
